title: crepidomanes schmidianum var. latifrons
intro: Crepidomanes schmidianum var. latifrons, also known as the Schmid's creeping fern or Schmid's climbing fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Crepidomanes schmidianum var. latifrons include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antidote for snake bites. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and crushed, then used as a tea or infused in hot water to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
